records.ety: [F. caresse, It. carezza, LL. caritia dearness, fr. L. carus dear. See Charity.]

defs: An act of endearment; any act or expression of affection; an embracing, or touching, with tenderness.
quotes: Wooed her with his soft caresses.
He exerted himself to win by indulgence and caresses the hearts of all who were under his command.

records.ety: [F. caresser, fr. It. carezzare, fr. carezza caress. See Caress., n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Caressed (k*rst"); p. pr. & vb. n. Caressing.]
usage: -- Caress, Fondle. “We caress by words or actions; we fondle by actions only.”

defs: To treat with tokens of fondness, affection, or kindness; to touch or speak to in a loving or endearing manner; to fondle.
quotes: The lady caresses the rough bloodhound.
